plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad market stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation by reducing leverage and concentration.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. Returns will likely be modest but more stable.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to address concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged and below stop.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 8% due to its downtrend, while maintaining some chip exposure.
Buy AMD at 5% as a high-scoring long setup to add quality tech.
Increase QQQI to 20% to balance growth with income and stability.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing new high-quality setups. Expect moderate drawdowns with potential for steady growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL completely as it is a flagged holding below its stop.
Increase SPAL to 5% to maintain leveraged exposure in a high-beta sector.
Trim SMH to 5% due to its downtrend, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Increase CRWD to 18% to press a winning position with strong momentum.
Buy AMD at 10% as a high-scoring long setup to add aggressive tech exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, stretching for growth. It increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. This mode carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining broad exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure completely.
Sell SMH to remove high-beta semiconductor exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to minimize high-beta single name risk.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation and stability.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, focusing on yield and capital preservation. It significantly reduces high-beta and leveraged holdings, emphasizing income-generating ETFs. While aiming for reliable distributions, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are not guaranteed.
